# Device Status Tracking

> Continuously monitor whether GPS devices are actively reporting, and automatically transition them between online, offline, and unknown states based on configurable inactivity thresholds, emitting notifications when devices go silent.

**Category:** Data · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · device-status · connectivity · fleet · monitoring

## What this does

Continuously monitor whether GPS devices are actively reporting, and automatically transition them between online, offline, and unknown states based on configurable inactivity thresholds, emitting notifications when devices go silent.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **status** *(select, required)*
- **last_update** *(datetime, optional)*
- **inactivity_start** *(number, optional)*
- **inactivity_period** *(number, optional)*

## What must be true

- **processing → online_on_position_received:** Status transitions to online as soon as a new position is received, regardless of current status
- **processing → offline_on_inactivity_threshold:** Status transitions to offline when (current_time - last_update) > inactivity_start
- **processing → threshold_inheritance:** If inactivity_start is not set on the device, it inherits the value from the parent group or server default
- **processing → check_schedule:** The inactivity check runs on a configurable schedule (typically every few minutes); status is not updated in real time between check runs
- **processing → repeated_inactivity_events:** If inactivity_period is set, repeated inactivity events are generated each time the silence duration increases by another inactivity_period interval
- **event_emission → transitions_trigger_notifications:** Status transitions are recorded as events and can trigger notifications to fleet users

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Device Remains Inactive** — when device continues to be silent beyond additional inactivity_period intervals; inactivity_period gt 0, then Repeated inactivity event emitted at each period boundary while device remains silent.
- **Device Goes Offline** — when scheduler determines that (now - last_update) > inactivity_start; status eq "online", then Device is marked offline; alert handlers can dispatch notifications to users.
- **Device Comes Online** — when device transmits a new position; status neq "online", then Device status is updated to online and subscribers are notified.

## Errors it can return

- `DEVICE_STATUS_NOT_FOUND` — The specified device does not exist

## Connects to

- **gps-device-registration**
- **gps-position-ingestion**
- **device-alarm-notifications**
